pr.title: lb: match fn_80026650 (100%), improve fn_800268B4 and lb_8000FD48
pr.author: itsgrimetime

summary: Matched lb audio function fn_80026650 to 100% and made small matching improvements to fn_800268B4 and lb_8000FD48. The audio changes replaced access through the lbl_80433710 struct anchor with direct named extern arrays lbl_804338A4 and lbl_80433984, and rewrote one loop to use pointer increments. The dynamics change factored repeated free-list pop logic into a static inline popDynamicsData, mirroring an existing inline pattern and improving generated code.

key_files.reason: Matched fn_80026650 and improved fn_800268B4 by changing extern/global access and loop form.
Improved lb_8000FD48 by extracting repeated DynamicsData free-list pop code into a static inline helper.

what_changed: In fn_80026650, array pointers arr_38a4 and arr_3984 now use direct named externs lbl_804338A4 and lbl_80433984 instead of fields lbl_80433710.x194 and lbl_80433710.x274.
fn_80026650 was reported as improving from 78.6% to a 100% match.
In fn_800268B4, the same named-extern swap was applied for arr_38a4 and arr_3984.
fn_800268B4 loop structure changed from indexed array accesses to pointer-dereference plus post-body pointer increments for arr_38a4, arr_3984, and arr_4e4.
fn_800268B4 loop condition changed from i > 0 to i != 0, supporting a CTR-loop/prologue layout noted in the PR summary.
fn_800268B4 was reported as improving from 88.5% to 88.7%.
Added static inline struct DynamicsData* popDynamicsData(void), which pops cur_data from a free list, clears entry->next, and returns NULL when no entry exists.
lb_8000FD48 now calls popDynamicsData for the first and subsequent entries instead of duplicating the free-list pop sequence inline.
lb_8000FD48 was reported as improving from 93.7% to 94.2%.
smart_moves: Using direct named externs for lbl_804338A4 and lbl_80433984 avoided forcing accesses through the broader lbl_80433710 struct anchor, which produced better codegen for fn_80026650 and fn_800268B4.
The pointer-increment form in fn_800268B4 aligned the C source with the expected assembly traversal pattern, including the reported bdnz/mtctr CTR-loop behavior.
Extracting popDynamicsData as a static inline preserved the repeated free-list semantics while giving the compiler a reusable shape similar to an existing inlineA0 pattern.
The PR verified both whole-project build health with python configure.py && ninja and per-function match percentages with tools/checkdiff.py.
decomp_lessons: When a global struct anchor causes poor matching, try naming and using the underlying extern arrays directly if evidence supports that layout.
For fixed-count array walks, pointer increments may match assembly better than indexing, especially when the target uses CTR loops and repeated dereferences.
Small source-level loop condition changes such as i > 0 versus i != 0 can matter for PowerPC loop/prologue generation.
Repeated logic can sometimes be improved for matching by extracting a static inline helper, particularly when it mirrors an already-successful inline pattern elsewhere in the file.
For free-list operations, clearing the popped node's next pointer inside the helper made the intended mutation explicit while preserving behavior.
naming_conventions: Introduced or used descriptive helper name popDynamicsData for popping struct DynamicsData entries from cur_data.
Kept temporary pointer names arr_38a4, arr_3984, and arr_4e4 tied to their underlying address/offset-derived global names.
Used direct global labels lbl_804338A4 and lbl_80433984 where those labels better described actual storage than lbl_80433710.x194 and lbl_80433710.x274.
No reviewer-driven naming feedback was present in the provided PR slice.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Replace struct-field global accesses with direct named extern array accesses when the assembly appears to reference the arrays independently.
Rewrite indexed loops into pointer-walk loops using *ptr and ptr++ to encourage matching load/update order.
Use i != 0 for countdown loops when trying to encourage CTR-loop-like codegen.
Extract repeated code into a static inline helper when the inline boundary and expression shape better match the compiler's expected output.
Validate changes using tools/checkdiff.py per touched function after confirming the full build still succeeds.
review_feedback: No review comments were present in the provided context.
The only human text evidence was the PR summary and verification notes.
follow_up_queries: Find other uses of lbl_80433710 fields that may match better as direct named extern arrays.
Search for countdown indexed loops in lbaudio_ax.c that could improve with pointer-increment traversal.
Compare popDynamicsData with the existing inlineA0 pattern mentioned in the PR summary for reusable inline matching style.
Audit other cur_data free-list pop sequences in lb_00F9.c for duplicated logic or matching opportunities.
